figures.caption: Fig. 5: An input instance from the COBOLEval benchmark.
Table 1: Effectiveness of COBOLAssist across different LLMs on COBOLEval. Zero-shot refinement refers to iterative self-correction without compiler feedback.

Fig. 6: Compilation Error Categorization of Human-Written and LLMgenerated COBOL Code (Before and After Using COBOLAssist). (*) The categories shown in this figure are grouped and renamed from the original categories presented in prior work (Litecky, 1974) to align with our classification scheme. Details on the mapping process can be found in the artifact.

Fig. 3: An example of Incorrect Use of Built-in Functions. The figure presents a COBOL snippet that incorrectly applies the MOD function as an inline operator rather than using the correct syntax FUNCTION MOD(arg1 arg2).
Fig. 7: Impact of COBOLAssist iteration configuration on execution time and pass@1 across different models.
Fig. 4: Overview of COBOLAssist—a self-debugging technique with compilation feedback.
Fig. 2: A COBOL program with two LINKAGE SECTION declarations, which violates COBOL’s structural rules, leading to a syntax error at line 41.

Table 2: Effectiveness of COBOLAssist in reducing compilation errors across different models. The table reports the number of compilation errors before and after applying COBOLAssist, the corresponding percentage reduction, and the average number of iterations required for correction. For mAInframer34B, COBOLAssist is performed using GPT-4o for the debugging process.
